note: Curated list of candidate mills / weavers / fabric manufacturers (NOT resellers of finished goods). Each entry must be verified directly with the supplier before placing orders — capabilities, MOQs, contact emails and certifications change over time. Where a public email could not be confirmed, contact_email is null and the inquiry should be sent via contact_page.

suppliers.name: Maeko Tessuti
suppliers.moq_notes: Low MOQ — friendly to small/emerging brands. Confirm exact MOQ per quality.
suppliers.ethical_principles: Traceable supply chain, natural fibres, transparent sourcing.

suppliers.name: Taroni S.p.A.
suppliers.moq_notes: Couture-level MOQ (typically 30–50 m per quality / colour). Confirm directly.
suppliers.verify_notes: Historic Como silk weaver, supplies haute couture. Confirm current mommi range and dyeing services.
suppliers.ethical_principles: Italian textile tradition; verify sustainability claims.

suppliers.name: Mantero Seta S.p.A.
suppliers.moq_notes: B2B — typical MOQ 50–100 m per quality. Confirm directly.
suppliers.verify_notes: Como silk house with vertically integrated dyeing and printing.
suppliers.ethical_principles: ZDHC participant; verify current sustainability programme.

suppliers.name: Ratti S.p.A.
suppliers.moq_notes: B2B — high MOQ (100+ m). Confirm directly.
suppliers.verify_notes: Vertically integrated, sustainability programme. Confirm cotton fibre origin.

suppliers.name: Canepa S.p.A.
suppliers.verify_notes: Como silk weaver, known for sustainable finishing (Kitotex).

suppliers.name: Belinac
suppliers.ethical_principles: European production; verify sourcing of raw silk.

suppliers.name: Albini Group
suppliers.moq_notes: Standard B2B: ~100+ m per article. Smaller MOQs possible via Albini Donna programme.
suppliers.verify_notes: Premium cotton shirting mill, owns Thomas Mason and David & John Anderson. Confirm dyeing capacity for custom colours.

suppliers.name: Canclini 1925
suppliers.verify_notes: Italian shirting mill, also offers Tencel blends.

suppliers.name: TINTEX Textiles
suppliers.moq_notes: Accommodating to emerging brands — typically 50–150 kg per article. Confirm directly.
suppliers.verify_notes: Naturally Advanced programme. Tencel jersey/woven specialist with in-house dyeing.
suppliers.ethical_principles: Cradle to Cradle Gold, closed-loop production, strong sustainability credentials.

suppliers.name: Riopele
suppliers.moq_notes: B2B — typically 200+ m per article. Confirm directly.
suppliers.verify_notes: Vertically integrated Portuguese mill, Tencel blends and dyeing.
suppliers.ethical_principles: Water reduction programme; verify current targets.

suppliers.name: Lemar S.p.A.
suppliers.verify_notes: Italian jersey mill working with natural fibres including Tencel.

suppliers.name: Lenzing AG (TENCEL fibre producer)
suppliers.moq_notes: Fibre producer — not a fabric MOQ. Use TENCEL E-Branding partner finder to locate authorised mills.
suppliers.verify_notes: Source of TENCEL/Lyocell fibre; not a fabric supplier itself. Use Lenzing E-Branding for licensed mills.
suppliers.ethical_principles: Closed-loop solvent recovery, certified wood pulp sourcing.

suppliers.name: Litrax
suppliers.moq_notes: Fibre supplier — connects you with mills that spin/weave Litrax bamboo. Confirm MOQ via Litrax.
suppliers.verify_notes: Offers mechanical (not viscose) bamboo fibre. Verify current availability and downstream mills.
suppliers.ethical_principles: Mechanical processing (closer to true 'natural' bamboo than bamboo viscose).

suppliers.name: Lanificio Paoletti / Tessitura Paoletti
suppliers.moq_notes: Small-batch friendly historic mill — relatively low MOQ. Confirm directly.
suppliers.verify_notes: Verify silk product range and current dyeing services.

suppliers.name: Tessitura Grisotto
suppliers.verify_notes: Como-area silk weaver. Dyeing typically outsourced to local dye houses.
suppliers.ethical_principles: Italian textile district; verify chain partners.
